column1: Major Rule Systems in the Bible and Judaism
Below is a structured overview of the primary rule systems found in the Bible and the major legal systems that developed in Judaism, presented in a timeline table for clarity.

column3: Core biblical laws, including the Ten Commandments and 613 mitzvot (commandments). Examples: Sabbath, dietary laws, prohibitions on idolatry, theft, adultery, etc.154
Additional laws in Leviticus, Deuteronomy, and Numbers: e.g., not mixing fabrics, agricultural rules, ritual purity.14
Reaffirmation and public reading of Torah law; beginnings of oral traditions.
First major written redaction of the Jewish Oral Law, organizing laws into six orders (Sedarim).
Expansive commentary on the Mishnah, including legal debates, stories, and further rules (Halakha).
Responsa and legal summaries from the Geonim (Babylonian Jewish leaders), clarifying Talmudic law.7
Major legal codes: Mishneh Torah (Maimonides, 12th c.), Tur (Jacob ben Asher, 14th c.), Shulchan Aruch (Joseph Caro, 16th c.).711
Ongoing legal decisions (responsa) and adaptations for new circumstances; modern reforms and state law (e.g., in Israel).7
